verdict: extend SubagentOrchestrator via new OrchestrationQueue layer above it

rejected.workflow-registry: read-only display of CC-owned state, no write/claim API

evidence: subagent-orchestrator.ts delegateRun:510 background:653 recoverInterruptedRuns:243; cwd already per-run via buildSubagentProviderRunForChat agent.ts:3084

verdict: worktree primitives already exist: worktree-store.adapter.ts addWorktree:97 removeWorktree:64 isDirty:50; per-file atomic commit exists diff-store.ts:2062 (git commit --only); conflict model = branch-per-worker, merge-tree dry-run at merge time
risks: resolveSubagentRoots rejects absolute workingDir - inject cwd directly instead

verdict: event-store approach: new orch-tasks.jsonl log, one event per state transition, writeChain serialization makes synchronous claim atomic in single process; tool-callback.ts recoverOnStartup:294 is the recovery precedent

mirror workflows WS pattern: new SubscriptionTopic {type:orchestration}, snapshot push + RPC drill-in (orchestration.getTask/getWorkerLog), zustand store w/ stable EMPTY, transcript launch card joins by id like WorkflowMessage taskId
open: snapshot-only vs snapshot+events for heartbeat frequency

note: c3 drift: c3-232 teams-registry documented but absent from main code (ADR 20260708 likely unmerged branch)

decisions.F2_restart: re-queue with progress kept, owner cleared
decisions.F3_permits: own configurable permit pool, separate from subagent maxParallel
decisions.F4_phases: fresh spawn per phase, split-context adversarial review by construction

note: Plan A of 3 (core engine). 12 tasks, TDD, self-reviewed (4 fixes: iterator narrowing, unused import, adapter draft cleanup, recovery spread). Plan B = real workers + MCP launch; Plan C = WS+UI.

decisions.F5_gates: engine gates in Plan A: gated state + gate events + injected approval port; hard gate pauses between phases (AG1-safe); soft gate = observable flag, continues; UI wiring Plan B/C
decisions.F6_atomicity: scopePaths on task spec, overlap = soft warn event
decisions.F7_trigger: main agent decomposes -> hard launch gate -> start_orchestration MCP tool (Plan B); panel form Plan C

note: F5/F6/F7 folded into Plan A in place: gated state + gate events + resolveGate/awaitGate + onGateOpened port + gated restart re-arm (resume with persisted phase output) + scopePaths soft overlap flag + Task 6b test suite. Trigger flow (F7) documented, lands Plan B.

decisions.F8_timeline: retain per-run event timeline in memory (OrchRunRecord.eventLog + getOrchRunEvents) for rich drill-in; rebuilt by replay
decisions.F9_handback: real workers signal hand-back via mcp__kanna__orch_hand_back({reason}) MCP tool (Plan B)
decisions.F10_transcripts: WorkerResult.subagentRunId -> orch_phase_completed.workers[] links panel to existing subagent transcript viewer

note: F8/F10 folded into Plan A: OrchRunRecord.eventLog + getOrchRunEvents + replay-rebuild test (Task 2, now 6 tests); WorkerResult.subagentRunId + orch_phase_completed.workers[]; F9 orch_hand_back MCP tool documented as Plan B scope. Push-model worker lifecycle documented in review reply.

decisions.F11_context: OrchRunConfig.contextPrompt injected into every worker prompt + scopePaths scope line injected into implement-phase prompts (Bun PORTING.md pattern; real example shown + confirmed)
decisions.F12_verify: mechanical verify after final phase before commit: config.verify {command,timeoutMs,retries}; fail -> fix-phase retry with verify output as PRIOR; exhausted -> task_failed; orch_verify_* events; engine reads exit code (no worker self-certification)

note: F11/F12 folded into Plan A: contextPrompt field on OrchRunConfig + scopePaths on OrchTaskRecord; composePrompt injects contextPrompt prefix on all prompts + scope hint on implement-kind phases; runVerifyLoop method after final phase with fix-phase retry + orch_verify_* events; runVerify injected dep; getOrchTaskPrompt renamed to getOrchTaskSpec returning {prompt,scopePaths}; all makeConfig() fixtures updated; Task 6c test suite added (5 verify tests + 3 F11 context/scope tests); verification table + CLAUDE.md section updated.

decisions.F13_worktree_pool: worktree pool pattern replaces worktree-per-task: config.worktreePoolSize worktrees pre-created + env-inited at createRun (branch orch/<runId>/wt-<i>); workers NOT pinned - scheduler claims task + free slot atomically (sync-apply, single event-loop turn = thread safety); commits from multiple tasks stack on the worktree's branch; {{DIFF}} anchored at per-claim baseSha not baseBranch; slot released on committed/failed, kept HELD across handed_back/requeue so dirty progress survives (F2); terminal failure resets uncommitted junk before release [ASSUMED policy]
decisions.F14_delivery: PR per worktree: N pool worktrees = N PRs; worktrees NOT removed at run end - user inspects and triggers PR creation per worktree (Plan B gh port + Plan C button); satisfies action-envelope PR approval
decisions.amendments: A initCommand accepted (runs once per pool worktree at provision, orch_worktree_init_* events); B per-phase provider/model on OrchPhaseSpec accepted; C reshaped to F14; D = no auto-cleanup, user decides

note: F13/F14 + amendments A/B folded into Plan A: worktreePoolSize + init on OrchRunConfig; provider/model on OrchPhaseSpec; OrchWorktreeSlot + pool fold (provisioned/init events, hold kept across requeue, released on terminal); claim event carries baseSha (diff anchor, slotHeads runtime map keeps claims synchronous); provisionPool at createRun; slot-gated scheduler with takenThisTurn guard + unusable-pool fail-fast; runTask drops per-task ensureWorktree, resetHard scrubs terminal failures, commit advances slotHeads; recovery rebuilds pool via idempotent ensureWorktree; adapter headSha+resetHard; tests: pool provisioning, never-share-slot concurrency, requeue-own-slot, failure reset, e2e worktrees-survive + provisioned trail; verification table F13/F14/A/B rows; CLAUDE.md + Plan B/C scope updated (PR-per-worktree user-triggered, run summary via origin-chat wake, UI: cancel/PR buttons, overlap badge, verify timeline, full creation form).

Starved-permit soft warn folded in: orch_config_warning event (observability-only fold) emitted at createRun when maxParallelTasks > worktreePoolSize; Task 6b test; verification row. Effective parallelism = min(permits, pool).
